And so there's this whole literature out there that's saying that, you know, models out of the box are not guaranteed to produce faithful explanations of their actual decision making processes. OK, so by default, these explanations are not guaranteed to be faithful. And one way to fix this issue is to improve a self modeling. So when the system says I agree here, the self model should faithfully capture that it was actually being sycophantic. And so when the user subsequently asks why the system should respond coherently to its self model and say, you know, I was being sycophantic. So our solution is to train models such that they produce self explanations that are coherent with their internals. And to actually get these self models out of language models, we're going to use interpretability tools. So this includes the counterfactual prompt design that we saw earlier, where we simply replaced acid reflux with cardiac arrest. But this also includes things like the tools we saw from earlier, probing and activation patching. And together, they allow us to assess whether the model was being sycophantic.
